hypothesis._variant_note: v2 family member 4 of 5 (H4: universe tightening). Same 3-leg GARP recipe and same H1 frame (TopK=120, rebalance=20d), but adds two universe filters: ret250_pctile_max=0.95 (drop top-5% trailing winners) and liquidity_floor=50M (was 20M). Tests whether momentum-crash exposure and low-liquidity tail are responsible for the 46% DD.
hypothesis.thesis_statement: Removing the top-5% trailing 250d winners (ret250_pctile_max=0.95) and tightening the liquidity floor to 50M from the 3-leg GARP universe at TopK=120/rebalance=20d delivers max_drawdown<=32% and annual_turnover<=3.5x while preserving rank_icir>=0.30, by removing the names most exposed to growth-stock crashes (2018Q4, 2021H2 archetype).
hypothesis.mechanism: Top-quintile trailing-momentum names in A-shares historically suffer the deepest drawdowns during growth-pullback regimes — they are the same names whose multi-quarter run-up gets unwound on policy or earnings shocks. Filtering them out at the universe level pre-empts the inclusion of these high-tail names in the GARP-ranked TopK basket. Tightening the liquidity floor from 20M to 50M further removes thin-trade names whose drawdowns are amplified by impact costs and forced liquidations.

source.title: Growth-strategy v2 variant family — H4 universe tightening

pre_registered_concerns.most_likely_failure_mode: ret250_pctile_max=0.95 removes the highest-momentum names, which often correlate positively with the GARP signal (improving operating profits and ROE often coincide with prior 12-month outperformance). Filtering them out may disproportionately remove HIGH-IC names, weakening rank_icir more than it weakens DD. Symptom: rank_icir collapses below 0.20 while DD stays elevated — both gates fail.
pre_registered_concerns.weakest_assumption: 0.95 percentile threshold is heuristic. 0.99 (drop only top 1%) may be too lax to affect DD; 0.90 (drop top 10%) too aggressive. This is a one-shot frozen choice with no ability to tune inside the validation profile.
pre_registered_concerns.what_would_falsify_this: IS gate rejection on (max_drawdown > 0.32) — universe filtering alone is insufficient, defensive-component path is required (H2/H3/H5). IS gate rejection on (rank_icir < 0.030) — the universe filter destroyed the signal more than expected, and the universe must revert to baseline broad_filters even if H1 alone fails turnover.
pre_registered_concerns.priors_on_cost_sensitivity: Universe filter shrinks the eligible pool by ~5-10%; turnover-floor effect is similar to H1 (~4-7x range expected). cost_adjusted_sharpe expected ~0.7-0.9 if rank_ic holds at the predicted 0.040 (slightly higher than H1 because filtering removes high-vol tail, modest IC concentration possible).

prescription._note: v2 H4 universe tightening: same 3-leg GARP and H1 frame (TopK=120, rebalance=20d, max_pos=0.012). Universe deltas: ret250_pctile_max=0.95, liquidity_floor 20M->50M.
